name: Firebase Admin Python SDK

auth.notes: Service account JSON or Application Default Credentials (ADC) for GCP-hosted apps

performance.notes: REST API + gRPC; Firestore streaming requires client SDK (not Admin); real-time limited server-side

agent_readiness.notes: Server-side Firebase SDK for Python (not client SDK). import firebase_admin. from firebase_admin import credentials, firestore, auth, messaging. cred = credentials.Certificate('service-account.json'). firebase_admin.initialize_app(cred). db = firestore.client(). db.collection('users').document('user1').set({'name': 'Alice'}). doc = db.collection('users').document('user1').get(). auth.create_user(email='user@example.com'). auth.verify_id_token(token). messaging.send(Message(notification=Notification(title='Hello'), token=fcm_token)). Batch writes. Admin access bypasses security rules.

gotchas: Admin SDK bypasses Firestore Security Rules — use carefully in user-facing APIs
Real-time listeners not supported server-side (client SDK only)
Service account key files must be kept secure (not in git)
Firestore transaction isolation is snapshot reads — watch for stale reads
best_when: Server-side Firestore data management and admin operations
Custom auth token generation for Firebase clients
User management (create, update, delete Firebase Auth users)
avoid_when: Real-time listeners in backend (use client SDK or Cloud Functions instead)
User-facing APIs that should respect Security Rules (admin bypasses them)
Non-Firebase backends (Cloud Firestore SDK without Firebase is lighter)
summary: Firebase Admin Python SDK for server-side Firebase operations. firebase_admin.initialize_app(credentials.Certificate('sa.json')). firestore.client() for Firestore. auth.verify_id_token(token) for auth. messaging.send(Message()) for FCM. Batch writes. Admin bypasses Security Rules — use carefully. No real-time listeners server-side. Best for backend user management, push notifications, and Firestore admin operations.
